interpretation: At this high prior weight, the true lattice beats none and the spectrum-matched permutation, with smoother and more low-frequency learned latent temporal deltas on the true graph.

Oracle heat dynamics are accurate, but raw D_dX did not favor the true graph and lower lambda only partly reduced harm; graph-generated dynamics did not automatically translate into latent graph-prior utility.

Raw alignment strongly favors the true graph, but 5 epochs classifies as generic smoothing; at 20 epochs graph beats permuted, while at 50 epochs permuted catches up despite graph having smoother latent deltas.

The correlation graph is smoother than permuted and random controls in raw temporal changes, but graph prior rollout is worse than both none and permuted; raw alignment did not guarantee rollout gain.
